pattern: Zeroing an index at second t only saves you its current value plus t times its growth rate, so pick which indices to sacrifice - and in what order - with a knapsack over 'growth rate sorted ascending, larger time slots to faster growers'.
hints: Every second adds nums2[i] to nums1[i] everywhere, even to indices you already zeroed earlier - so zeroing late is worth more the faster that index grows.

For a fixed number of seconds T, decide which T indices to zero and in which order: the value 'saved' by zeroing index i at time slot t (1-indexed, 1..T) is nums1[i] + t * nums2[i]. To maximize total savings, pair the largest time slots with the largest growth rates - sort by nums2 ascending and greedily reason from there.

Sort indices by nums2 ascending. Run a 0/1-knapsack style DP over 'choose exactly k of the first i items', where taking item i as the k-th chosen contributes nums1[i] + k * nums2[i]. For each T from 0 to n, the minimal sum is sum(nums1) + T*sum(nums2) - dp[T]; return the smallest T where that is <= x, or -1 if none in [0, n] works.

edge_inputs.description: three indices, a moderate target reachable in three seconds
growth rates too high for any number of operations to reach the target
single index already under target with zero growth
single index that needs exactly one zeroing operation
target of zero is unreachable once more than one index grows
target already satisfied at time zero, no operations needed
zero growth rates make slot order irrelevant, this is a pure top-two selection
already below target despite enormous growth rates, so the answer must stay zero rather than over-zeroing
target of zero stays unreachable even after using every available operation

stress.description: 1000 indices with random growth rates and a random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums1 = [rng.randrange(1, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randrange(0, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    x = rng.randrange(0, 1000001)
    return [nums1, nums2, x]
stress.note: exercises the full O(n^2) DP at the constraint ceiling
